description: In October 2016, the board of Tata Sons suddenly removed Cyrus Mistry as chairman, sparking a public and legal battle for control of the $100 billion Indian conglomerate. The incident divided the Indian business establishment between supporters of the traditional Tata family values and supporters of Mistry's modernization efforts.

key_events.description: Cyrus Mistry is unceremoniously removed by the Tata Sons board.
Mistry sends a letter to the board alleging governance failures.
The Tata group initiates removal of Mistry from individual group company boards.
Shareholder meetings confirm the removal of Mistry from key subsidiaries.

calibration_notes: Models the dynamics of long-term internal corporate power struggles and governance disputes.
